name: Roast Peppers Stuffed with Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C/400°F (gas mark 6).
2. Halve the peppers length-wise. Remove the core and seeds. Place cut-side down on a baking sheet and roast for 15 minutes, turning frequently.
3. Meanwhile, prepare the tomato sauce: If using fresh tomatoes, place them in a bowl, add boiling water to cover, and leave for 15-30 seconds. Transfer to cold water, then remove and peel. Roughly chop.
4. Finely chop the onion. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan, add the onion and garlic, and fry until softened and lightly colored. Add chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, muscovado sugar, salt, and pepper. Cook uncovered, stirring for 15-20 minutes or until reduced to a thick sauce.
5. Thoroughly slice mushrooms. Heat the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan and sauté the mushrooms until soft.
6. Stir the mushrooms, nuts, marjoram, and olives into the tomato mixture. Fill the peppers with the stuffing and top with grated Parmesan.
7. Roast in the oven for 15-20 minutes or until heated through.
8. Serve one red and one yellow pepper half per person. Garnish with salad leaves and herbs.
